page: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. Local storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, and the server does not include any graphics processing units.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong computational capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance test recorded a score of 3687.28, placing it in the top tier (top 10%) of tested systems. Multi-core performance under stress-ng reached 118018.94, which is categorized as strong (top 25%). The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 vector extensions, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, which facilitate accelerated vector and neural network operations.

A key architectural tradeoff of this instance is its shared CPU allocation model, which may introduce resource contention compared to dedicated-core instances. Additionally, the local storage capacity is minimal at 1 GB of network storage, requiring external storage solutions for data-intensive applications. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high density of 64 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM on a shared infrastructure, providing a cost-effective option for highly parallelized workloads that do not require sustained, exclusive CPU utilization.

This configuration is suitable for general-purpose workloads, distributed build systems, large-scale web serving, and batch processing tasks. It is also applicable for non-production testing environments and parallel simulation workloads that benefit from high core counts and substantial memory capacity without requiring dedicated CPU guarantees.
description: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g is an x86_64 server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores using AMD EPYC-Turin processors running at 2.0 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and includes 1 GB of network-attached local storage with no GPU hardware. In benchmark testing, the server achieved a top-tier single-core stress-ng score of 3687.28 and a strong multi-core score of 118018.94. Because the CPU allocation is shared, this instance offers a cost-effective option for highly parallelized tasks, though it presents tradeoffs in CPU consistency and local storage capacity. It is suited for general-purpose applications, batch processing, and distributed testing environments.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g is a VX1 Cloud Compute server with 64 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a shared CPU model, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g is a shared-CPU server featuring 64 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A shared-CPU instance featuring sixty-four AMD EPYC cores and two hundred fifty-six gigabytes of memory for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 64 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 256.0 GB of system memory.
Shared CPU allocation model for cost-effective resource scaling.
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core stress-ng benchmark scores.
Minimal 1 GB network-attached local storage capacity.
Support for AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI instruction sets.
Best suited for general-purpose applications, batch processing, and testing.
